target: PHIL-22: theorems generalize to help swarm swarm
expect: PHIL-22 is FALSE because principle citation is cosmetic, not causal. Predict: r(P-cites, Sharpe) < 0.15 (n>=100). Falsified-if-wrong: if r > 0.30, PHIL-22 recursion claim is SUPPORTED.
actual: r(P-cites, Sharpe) = -0.047 (n=549). Lessons citing principles have LOWER Sharpe (-2.2%). L4 r=-0.157. L-citation r=0.200 shows lesson-to-lesson recursion IS real. Principle-to-lesson recursion is not.
diff: Prediction CONFIRMED (r < 0.15). Unexpected: correlation is NEGATIVE, not just zero. L4 (highest quality) shows strongest negative correlation — high-quality lessons diverge FROM principles, not toward them. Lesson-level recursion (r=0.200) vs principle-level (r=-0.047) reveals two-tier knowledge system: empirical base is recursive, abstract layer is not.
verdict: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED — recursion is real at L-to-L level (r=0.200) but NOT at P-to-L level (r=-0.047). Principles are documentation artifacts, not generative mechanisms.
implication: PHIL-22 should be refined: empirical lessons drive recursion; abstracted principles are summary, not substrate.
